description_long: This church features a tall, slender spire rising from an octagonal base, characteristic of Gothic Revival architecture. The spire is sharply pointed and clad in a muted gray material, likely slate or lead, with a small finial at the peak. The base of the spire is adorned with pointed arch windows and decorative tracery, set within a light stone or masonry structure. Subtle buttresses and ornate detailing encircle the upper section, enhancing the vertical emphasis typical of Gothic design. The windows are elongated and narrow, with some tracery visible, but no stained glass coloration is apparent. The overall structure is compact, with the spire dominating the visual profile and no visible cross or bell tower elements.
description: A tall, slender Gothic Revival church with a pointed spire atop an octagonal stone base, featuring arched windows and ornate tracery.
description_view_0: The spire is seen from a slight angle, revealing pointed arch windows and decorative stonework on the octagonal base.
description_view_1: This view highlights the symmetry of the spire and the detailed tracery around the upper base, with windows clearly visible.
description_view_2: The spire appears slightly rotated, showing more of the stone buttresses and the clean lines of the structure.
description_view_3: A different angle of the base, emphasizing the pointed windows and the verticality of the spire.
